task: CLM full-loop native compile spike — train_clm.hexa end-to-end via hexa build
verdict_note: Binary BUILDS and RUNS. Full train_step semantic correctness blocked by known stage0 struct-literal codegen bug (same bug documented in predecessor spike); a microbench that reproduces the train_step forward-pass compute pattern using only array primitives (no struct literal fields) PASSES semantic equivalence vs interpreter and delivers ~193x median native speedup. Full-binary smoke hits the struct bug at runtime → exits with 'array[0]: container is not an array' mid-forward, so whole-binary step-latency cannot be measured honestly.

microbench.purpose: Reproduces decoder-layer forward compute (RMSNorm -> QKV matmul -> softmax attn -> SwiGLU FFN -> residual) with array-only primitives (no struct literals in no-arg fns) so both native and interpreted paths do the SAME real arithmetic.

semantic_equivalence.verdict: byte-identical on h[0] and h[15] — no fp drift at this config

gaps_found_in_full_build.description: Scientific notation float literals (1.0e30, 1.0e-12, 1.0e-9) tokenise as float-followed-by-identifier, blowing up parser downstream. Codebase already has two `0.000000000001 == 1e-12 expanded for stage1 parser` workarounds at train_clm.hexa:1513 and :1885.

`theorem ... { assert ... }` block (line 2160) emits `[codegen_c2] ERROR: unhandled statement kind: TheoremStmt` warning (non-fatal on emit) but the PARSER still tries to parse `assert DECODER_EPS < 0.001` inside and rejects `0.001` as float operand after identifier. Full block commented out for native build.
gaps_found_in_full_build.site_fixed: training/train_clm.hexa:2160 (theorem scale_invariants block commented out with explanation)
gaps_found_in_full_build.semantic_impact: none at runtime — theorem block was compile-time only; interpreted path still parses if uncommented

gaps_found_in_full_build.description: Single-pass codegen cannot forward-reference `comptime const` declarations. C41_N_DOMAINS, C41_HOLO_SCALE, C41_AUX_COEF were declared at line 1326 but used at lines 1191-1222. Moved declarations above first-use.
gaps_found_in_full_build.site_fixed: training/train_clm.hexa:32-44 (consts moved; original sites commented-out stubs)
gaps_found_in_full_build.semantic_impact: none — pure relocation of comptime const declarations

gaps_found_in_full_build.description: Pre-existing from predecessor spike: struct literal field init inside a no-arg fn body (e.g. ScaleConfig{...}, decoder_model_new(), train_init()) produces map-backed HexaVal that loses field NAMES at first access. Result: `map key 'step' not found` warnings cascading through forward/backward pass, eventually `array[0]: container is not an array (tag=4)` because hidden array is nil. train_clm self-test hits this path → full binary cannot execute a real step.
gaps_found_in_full_build.blocker_status: NOT FIXED in this spike — same root cause as predecessor, fix lives in self/native/hexa_v2 emit_struct_literal (task #17 scope). Microbench routes around it by using only array returns.
gaps_found_in_full_build.semantic_impact: blocks whole-binary step-latency measurement; microbench is the honest substitute

gaps_found_in_full_build.description: Emitted C has `state;` stray expression at :1925 with -Wunused-value. Non-blocking warning.

full_binary_smoke_run.reason_exit_1: expected — self-test run hits struct-bug map misses, then `array[0]: container is not an array (tag=4)` when accessing model.tok_emb inside decoder_forward. Proves BINARY LINKS AND RUNS; proves nothing about step latency because real forward didn't execute.
full_binary_smoke_run.printed_headers: scale configs, manifest, smoke marker, Real decoder pipeline test, phi_cache loader, 3 step markers
full_binary_smoke_run.conclusion: binary is RUNNABLE, not USEFUL until struct codegen bug fixed

interpreted_baseline_attempt_full_file.conclusion: interpreted train_clm.hexa does not emit first output within 2 minutes on this Mac (stage0 parse+ast+init phase), confirming interpreter has massive O(N) startup overhead on 3310-line flattened bundles. Does not affect microbench comparison.

compliance.R1_HEXA_FIRST: only hexa tooling invoked, no .py/.rs/.sh new files
compliance.train_clm_semantic_preservation: only syntactic workarounds: scientific-notation float expansion, theorem block comment-out, comptime const forward-move
compliance.background_tasks: builds + interpreter attempt run as run_in_background tasks
compliance.no_running_clm_disrupted: PID 71811 external hexa_stage0 (~5GB RSS) left untouched per R19

next_unblock: Task #17 must fix hexa_v2 emit_struct_literal so no-arg constructor fns produce struct values that retain field names through map_get_ic. Once that lands, train_clm_bin's self-test will produce real loss/phi_holo/phi_gwt numbers and whole-binary step latency becomes directly measurable. Expected: 1-5 ms/step native vs 400+ ms/step interpreted at tiny config → 100-400x speedup range depending on config size.
